title: Running rootless without changing the user ID is not working
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
When running:
```
podman run --userns=keep-id --storage-opt ignore_chown_errors --annotation io.crun.keep_original_groups=1 --user 0 -i -t ubuntu:latest
```
 I always get the following error when trying to run Podman:
`Error processing tar file(exit status 1): potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 0:42 for /etc/gshadow): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid: lchown /etc/gshadow: invalid argument`


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `cat ~/.config/containers/storage.conf`
```
[storage]  
driver = "overlay"  
runroot = "$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR"  
graphroot = "/scratch/tmp/$USER/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
```
2. `podman run --userns=keep-id --storage-opt ignore_chown_errors --annotation io.crun.keep_original_groups=1 --user 0 -i -t ubuntu:latest`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
ERRO[0000] cannot find UID/GID for user <user>: No subuid ranges found for user "<user>" in /etc/subuid - check rootless mode in man pages. 
Completed short name "ubuntu" with unqualified-search registries (origin: /etc/containers/registries.conf)
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubuntu:latest...
  name unknown: Repo not found
Trying to pull registry.redhat.io/ubuntu:latest...
  unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password: unauthorized: Please login to the Red Hat Registry using your Customer Portal credentials. Further instructions can be found here: https://access.redhat.com/RegistryAuthentication
Trying to pull docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob a70d879fa598 done  
Copying blob 10e6159c56c0 done  
Copying blob c4394a92d1f8 done  
Copying config 26b77e5843 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
  Error processing tar file(exit status 1): potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 0:42 for /etc/gshadow): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid: lchown /etc/gshadow: invalid argument
Error: 3 errors occurred while pulling:
 * Error initializing source docker://registry.access.redhat.com/ubuntu:latest: Error reading manifest latest in registry.access.redhat.com/ubuntu: name unknown: Repo not found
 * Error initializing source docker://registry.redhat.io/ubuntu:latest: unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password: unauthorized: Please login to the Red Hat Registry using your Customer Portal credentials. Further instructions can be found here: https://access.redhat.com/RegistryAuthentication
 * Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:a70d879fa5984474288d52009479054b8bb2993de2a1859f43b5480600cecb24": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 0:42 for /etc/gshadow): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid: lchown /etc/gshadow: invalid argument
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
I would expect to keep all my user and group ID's as of the host system

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2
Go Version:   go1.14.12
Built:        Mon Feb 22 05:51:35 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.2.1-7.module_el8.3.0+699+d61d9c41.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
We would like to run Podman in a scientific HPC environment with our data residing on shared file systems (NFS and CephFS).
Also, we are using FreeIPA for user authentication.
Therefore, it is essential to keep the user and group ID's of the host system.
We have to avoid using user namespaces.

comments.body: seems like ignore chown errors isn't working, could you try forcing `ignore_chown_errors=true`: --storage-opt `ignore_chown_errors=true` ?

oh I see.  `--storage-opt` is an option to podman, not podman run:

```
podman --storage-opt ignore_chown_errors=true run --userns=keep-id --annotation io.crun.keep_original_groups=1 --user 0 -i -t ubuntu:latest
```

does it work better?

Thanks @giuseppe, this actually solved part of the problem:
```
> podman --storage-opt ignore_chown_errors=true run --userns=keep-id --annotation io.crun.keep_original_groups=1 --user 0 -i -t ubuntu:latest
Error: kernel does not support overlay fs: 'overlay' is not supported over xfs at "/scratch/tmp/<user>/containers/storage/overlay": backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver
```
(This error message is not really helpful. The issue is permissions-based, not because of xfs as a file system.)
Seems like specifying --storage-opt lets podman forget the mount_program:
```

> podman --storage-opt ignore_chown_errors=true --storage-opt mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs run --userns=keep-id --annotation io.crun.keep_original_groups=1 --user 0 -i -t ubuntu:latest
ERRO[0000] cannot find UID/GID for user <user>: No subuid ranges found for user "<user>" in /etc/subuid - check rootless mode in man pages. 
ERRO[0000] cannot find UID/GID for user <user>: No subuid ranges found for user "<user>" in /etc/subuid - check rootless mode in man pages. 
Error: chown /run/user/5534/containers/overlay-containers/ce24ae1e416da622dd405f4229f8338923df77c8dee81758c93d893bdece8e35/userdata: invalid argument
```

OK, the image is now downloaded. But why does podman still complain about subuid ranges?
As mentioned, I do not want to change the uid/gid namespace.
`--userns=keep-id` and `--user=0` are in conflict with each other.  With one just id available `--userns=keep-id` doesn't make much sense.  Could you drop it?

Same issue:
```
> podman --storage-opt ignore_chown_errors=true --storage-opt mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs run --userns=keep-id --annotation io.crun.keep_original_groups=1 -i -t ubuntu:latest 
ERRO[0000] cannot find UID/GID for user <user>: No subuid ranges found for user "<user>" in /etc/subuid - check rootless mode in man pages. 
Error: chown /run/user/5534/containers/overlay-containers/3a377522617e63f7931927aa84ae11d516d20cd0f38f92a65f3cad4bd151c5c2/userdata: invalid argument
```

I still see `--userns=keep-id` in the command above.  Could you try?

```
> podman --storage-opt ignore_chown_errors=true --storage-opt mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs run --annotation io.crun.keep_original_groups=1 -i -t ubuntu:latest 
```

Awesome, this works great, I do even have access to the shared file system using my own credentials, including ACLs :tada: 
Thanks a lot @giuseppe, his perfectly solved my issue!

---
One last question:
```
root@3cb7b8412a7e:/# id
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root),65534(nogroup)
```
Could you elaborate a bit on how permission conversion works here?
E.g.:
- Would I be able to change my primary group inside the container?
- Is there support for editing ACLs from inside the container?
- What about Kerberos tickets?

no, you won't be able to change the additional groups.  You can change them only to groups that are already mapped inside the user namespace, but you cannot use `setgroups()` for anything else.  Once you do that, you also lose access to the old groups that are not mapped inside the user namespace.
